questions.question: How many failed SSH logins came from IP 192.168.1.45?
questions.expected_answer: 6 failed SSH logins (3 as admin, 3 as root) before a successful login as admin

questions.question: What was the brute force attack pattern from 192.168.1.45?
questions.expected_answer: 6 failed SSH logins alternating between admin and root users within seconds (14:23:01 to 14:23:18), followed by a successful login as admin at 14:23:22, then a privilege escalation attempt at 14:30:00

questions.question: What malware was detected on 10.0.0.5 and what lateral movement technique was used?
questions.expected_answer: Cobalt Strike beacon was detected in /tmp/.hidden/payload.exe. Lateral movement via PsExec targeting 10.0.0.6 and 10.0.0.7

questions.question: What data exfiltration indicators were detected?
questions.expected_answer: 2.3GB data transfer from 172.16.0.100 to external IP 185.220.101.45, anomalous DNS query to data.exfil.evil.com, connection to known C2 server at 185.220.101.45

questions.question: What supply chain attack was detected and what CVE was involved?
questions.expected_answer: Malicious dependency in event-stream@5.0.0 package, and CVE-2024-3094 (xz-utils/sshd backdoor)

questions.question: What phishing attempt was detected and who was targeted?
questions.expected_answer: Phishing email to hr_inbox with subject 'Urgent: Update your credentials' from support@1egit-company.com (note the '1' instead of 'l')

questions.question: What is the current status of INC-2024-001?
questions.expected_answer: Closed. The ransomware attempt was contained, files restored from backup, attacker C2 blocked, and post-incident review completed with MFA enforced for all admin accounts.

questions.question: Which incident involved data exfiltration and how many customers were affected?
questions.expected_answer: INC-2024-002: Data exfiltration via compromised svc_backup service account. 2.3GB exfiltrated, breach notification sent to 15,000 affected customers.

questions.question: What APT group was attributed to the development infrastructure attack?
questions.expected_answer: INC-2024-003: TTPs matched APT29 (likely state-sponsored). The attack involved supply chain compromise (event-stream), crypto mining on CI server, DNS tunneling, and xz-utils backdoor (CVE-2024-3094).

questions.question: How was the AWS key exposure in INC-2024-004 resolved?
questions.expected_answer: Key immediately revoked, CloudTrail audit showed key was used 3 times before revocation but no customer data was accessed (only listed buckets). Git-secrets hook deployed to all repos with mandatory pre-commit scanning.

questions.question: Which incidents have CVEs associated with them?
questions.expected_answer: INC-2024-001 has CVE-2024-21626, and INC-2024-003 has CVE-2024-3094 (xz-utils backdoor)

questions.question: What was the timeline of the insider threat incident?
questions.expected_answer: INC-2024-006: DLP alert at 14:00 for bulk download of 500+ sensitive documents, account suspended at 14:30, HR/Legal notified and device confiscated at 15:00. User had resigned 2 weeks prior and was downloading competitor-sensitive data. Legal action initiated.
